facts.text: Mays Landing takes its name from Captain George May, who sailed the Great Egg Harbor River in 1740 and bought land there in 1756.
When Atlantic County was formed in 1837, Mays Landing was selected as its county seat and the courthouse was completed the following year.
The town began as an iron‑forge settlement in 1705 when William May, an iron master, established a forge on the Great Egg Harbor River.
The historic Atlantic County Courthouse, built in 1838, still stands as a landmark that symbolizes Mays Landing’s early civic life.
Early cotton mills in Mays Landing used railroads to import raw materials and export finished products, linking the town to regional trade.
Local timber and bog iron supplied early shipbuilding, making Mays Landing a key site for constructing vessels on the Great Egg Harbor River.
